rows.notes: Large, clear year heading at top of page; perfectly legible.
Centered title under the year; handwriting is clear and underlined.
Clear carried-forward amount at top right. The '481' in the pounds column, '0' in shillings and '7' in pence are well aligned with the ruling lines and unambiguous.

Description reads 'Subscriptions Yarnton School' (Yarnton parish fits context). Pounds column clearly '3'. Shillings and pence are faint; I read '3' shillings and a faint '1' in pence by alignment with ruling lines but the small pence digit is slightly smudged.

Parish name 'Medmenham do' is legible. Pounds '3' is clear. The shillings digit appears as '1' and pence as '3' but both are faint and could be mis-read; chosen by alignment with the vertical ruling lines.

Entry reads 'Garsington do'. Pounds '3' is clear. The shillings and pence appear to match the previous two small entries (read as '1' and '3'), but the ink is light; choice based on column alignment and pattern.

Description 'Merton do' is clear. Pounds column reads '10' (two-digit, clear). There is a small '5' visible in the pence column; the middle shillings column appears blank/zero. Ink smudge makes pence slightly uncertain but alignment supports 5d.
Reads 'Long Wittenham do'. Pounds '5' visible in pounds column; pence '5' visible in the pence column. Shillings blank/0. Ink slightly uneven, so moderate confidence.

Description reads 'Benson Do'. Pounds column shows '2'. The middle column shows '5' (shillings) and pence column shows '2' — these small digits are readable but slightly faded; assigned moderate confidence.

The place-name is unclear in the manuscript (reads something like 'Grogwear' or 'Grogen'). Pounds column clearly '5'. Shillings and pence appear blank. Because the description is uncertain, confidence is low.
Line reads 'Do. Poor' (likely same parish giving for the poor). Pounds column shows '10' clearly. Shillings/pence blank. Good alignment with ruling lines gives higher confidence for the amount.
Text reads 'Church Building Socy Diocesan' (abbreviated). Amount '5' in pounds column is clear; shillings/pence blank. Good alignment and legibility for amount.
Reads 'Widows and Orphans. Do' (do = ditto to previous parish/collection). Pounds '5' is clear; no shillings/pence marked.
Handwriting 'Easter Offering' is legible. In the three narrow columns I read a small '1' in pounds, a '1' in shillings and a faint '1' in pence — digits are small/faint, so moderate confidence.
Description reads 'Compensation Fellows Dr Price' (handwritten abbreviation). Pounds '22' clearly visible; shillings '7' and pence '6' readable though pence digit smaller. Column alignment is good.
Line reads 'Do Dr J. Newton' but there is a dash (—) or no amount written in the three narrow columns. I therefore record no amount; the text is legible but amount absent.
Reads 'Room Rent Balance Dir 1865' (balance for the year). The pounds '552' (three-digit) is clear and well aligned; shillings '2' and pence '3' are readable with good contrast.
Short entry 'Corn Prices'. Pounds column shows '2' clearly; no shillings or pence. Good alignment with ruling lines.

Entry begins 'Synodals. Merton & Tew...' with some abbreviations; the description is partly obscured by abbreviations. Amount reads as '1' in pounds and '15' in shillings (placed across the two shilling digits) — shillings digit cluster is small but fits 0–19 range.
Text reads 'Insurance Primage Janm' (handwritten abbreviation). Pounds '26' and shillings '11' are legible; pence blank. Slight ink blotting near the word but amounts are clear.
Description 'Porter Addl Salary (Chapel 5 - Library 10)' is readable. Pounds '15' is clear in the pounds column; no shillings/pence marked.
Short line 'Ley.' or 'Leg.' (abbreviation) is written; pounds column shows '100' clearly. Middle and pence columns blank. Description uncertain but amount legible.
Description 'Diocesan Board of Education' is legible. Pounds column '10' is clear; no shillings/pence. Good alignment and contrast.
Final line at bottom right reads '1229 13 1' (final total). The three-digit pounds and the shillings/pence digits are clearly written and aligned with the ruling lines.
